diagnosis: The level is a 300+ tile wide flat corridor with a solid floor at y=690px and only minor floating platforms near the top. The player spent the first 21 seconds stationary at spawn (x=176), then moved right in a straight line with zero vertical traversal — no ladders, climbs, or elevation changes. A single combat death occurred at x=2930 from a wasp enemy whose stinger persisted after death, which the player explicitly called out as unfair. Completion took 110s with 5 hits taken, indicating repetitive, low-engagement traversal punctuated by one frustrating enemy mechanic.

lessons.lesson: Persistent projectiles or hazards from dead enemies (e.g., stingers, bullets) that remain active after the enemy is killed create perceived unfairness, as players expect threat removal on kill.
conditions: enemy dies but its projectile/hazard remains active
player can be damaged by remnant hazard after kill
no visual cue that hazard persists
lessons.outcome: player takes damage they feel is unavoidable and unjust, rates level poorly, calls out mechanic explicitly
